news.summary: Chipmakers are potentially gearing up for a notable seasonal upswing, following a historical trend that could further boost their current rebound driven by positive U.S.-China trade sentiments and relaxed export constraints. The iShares Semiconductor ETF (NYSE: SOXX) monitors 34 leading U.S. chip companies and has typically shown significant growth in the second half of May. In the last decade, the ETF closed higher in 9 out of 10 years during this period, with an average return of 4.3%, as per Seasonax data. A notable surge was seen in 2023 when SOXX spiked 13% between May 15 and May 31. The only negative occurrence in the last decade was in 2019 when the ETF dropped by 10.7% during the same timeframe. Expanding the analysis to 20 years also confirms this pattern, with gains recorded in 14 out of 20 years and an average return of 2.8%. For more details, the full article can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Chipmakers entering a bullish seasonal window, with iShares Semiconductor ETF historically gaining 4.3% in the second half of May.

news.summary: A report titled "Rethinking Sustainability in Corporate America – 2025" provides insights from top company executives on sustainability and ESG practices. Released on May 8, 2025, in New York by AccountAbility, a reputable Consulting, Standards, and Research organization, the report aims to shed light on corporate sustainability strategies. The report includes perspectives from executives overseeing sustainability and ESG initiatives at leading companies. This comprehensive examination offers valuable insights into the current state of sustainability practices in corporate America.
news.headline: AccountAbility report highlights sustainability as a core business focus in Corporate America by 2025.

news.summary: The Golden Ocean Group Limited recently held its 2025 Annual General Meeting on May 8, 2025, at 08:30 hrs in Hamilton, Bermuda. news.summary: On May 8, 2025, in New York, the renowned PR and digital marketing agency, 5WPR, has revealed that it has secured the position of agency of record for everbowl. This notable quick-serve superfood chain is dedicated to advocating balanced living through its offerings. 5WPR, one of the largest independent PR and digital marketing firms in the United States, is set to spearhead the promotional efforts for everbowl to enhance its market presence and reach a wider audience with its message of wellness.
news.headline: 5WPR selected as agency for everbowl, a superfood chain, aiming to promote balanced living.

news.summary: Former Celsius CEO Alex Mashinsky has been sentenced to 12 years in prison by the US federal court for the Southern District of New York for fraud. Despite his legal team aiming for a lighter sentence due to his military service and guilty plea, prosecutors pushed for a 20-year term, with betting markets predicting a lighter sentence. This sentence contrasts with President Trump's previous pardons for crypto executives. The DOJ wants Mashinsky's sentence to serve as a warning to others in the cryptocurrency industry, emphasizing that fraud will be severely punished regardless of technology. Mashinsky targeted retail investors, leading to the prosecution arguing for a strong sentence. Mashinsky's defense highlighted his character but ultimately failed to sway the court. The article also mentions Trump's pardons for other crypto executives, indicating a trend of increased interest in presidential pardons among white-collar convicts in the crypto world. Mashinsky's sentence underscores the consequences for financial crimes in the evolving crypto landscape.
news.headline: Mashinsky sentenced to 12 years for fraud, despite predictions of a lighter term; Trump's admin warns crypto industry.

news.summary: In Knoxville, Tennessee, Pilot is supporting American Stroke Month by fundraising for the American Heart Association to promote stroke prevention and education. Between May 9 and 31, customers can contribute by rounding up their purchases at Pilot, Flying J, and One9 Travel Center stores across the country. The initiative aims to provide vital support for stroke prevention programs and resources, with all donations directly benefiting these efforts. This campaign underscores Pilot's commitment to raising awareness about strokes and supporting community health.
news.headline: Pilot marks Stroke Awareness Month by raising funds for the American Heart Association through round-up donations at checkout.

news.summary: A company known as Bakelite Synthetics revealed plans to endorse the Louisiana Tech University’s Forest Products Innovation Center as the primary Industry Partner for design and construction. This venture is significant in solidifying the company’s position as a leading entity in the industry, offering vital testing and research resources to students and professionals. Bakelite Synthetics will actively engage with the university's community, including students and faculty, strengthening collaborative efforts to meet industry demands.
news.headline: Bakelite Synthetics partners with Louisiana Tech University's Forest Products Innovation Center, serving as top industry partner.

news.summary: On Thursday, Sunrun Inc. (NASDAQ: RUN) saw a rise in its shares following a positive first-quarter earnings report. The company reported an EPS of $0.20 per share, surpassing the analyst consensus of a $0.25 loss. In terms of revenue, Sunrun reported $504.27 million, higher than the estimated $484.06 million. Although there was a modest customer growth volume in the quarter, with 25,428 additions (up 6%), significant deceleration in comparisons was expected. Guggenheim analyst Joseph Osha maintained a Neutral rating on the stock and highlighted that Sunrun is prioritizing liquidity and profitability over aggressive expansion in the near to medium term. Osha foresees a shift in focus when a more advantageous environment arises. Additionally, Jim Cramer remarked on Sunrun's recent performance while expressing confidence in ExlService Holdings. Osha projects further potential for Sunrun in the future.
news.headline: Sunrun Inc. (NASDAQ: RUN) beats Q1 estimates with EPS of 20 cents and revenue of $504.27 million, up 6% in customer volume growth. Guggenheim analyst maintains Neutral rating.

news.headline: Alex Mashinsky, former CEO of Celsius Network, sentenced to 12 years in prison after requesting a shorter term.

news.summary: A new biomass processing facility has been launched in Grand Rapids, Michigan by Woodchuck, catering to creating renewable energy from wood waste. This is the first AI-powered wood waste-to-energy facility in the US. Governor Gretchen Whitmer, along with local and state officials, clean energy advocates, and business leaders from Grand Rapids, marked the opening. The facility represents a significant advancement in the field and is expected to play a key role in addressing environmental challenges.
news.headline: Gov. Whitmer hails Woodchuck's biomass facility in Grand Rapids, MI, leading in clean energy; 1st AI wood waste-to-energy.

news.summary: In Birmingham, Alabama on May 8, 2025, Essnova Solutions, Inc. shared its excitement about securing the new OASIS Plus (One Acquisition Solution for Integrated Services Plus) contract from the U.S. General Services Administration (GSA). The contract, awarded in four categories, marks a significant achievement for Essnova in expanding its government contract portfolio.
news.headline: Essnova awarded GSA OASIS+ contract in four categories and five domains, marking a significant achievement.

news.summary: Entertainment company Warner Bros. Discovery, Inc. (NASDAQ: WBD) released its first-quarter fiscal results, showing a 9% revenue decline year-on-year to $8.98 billion, falling short of the $9.60 billion estimated by analysts. The EPS loss was 18 cents, missing the anticipated loss of 11 cents. The net loss was $453 million, down from $966 million the previous year. The adjusted EBITDA was $2.11 billion, reflecting a 4% growth. Distribution revenues saw a 1% decrease to $4.89 billion due to a drop in domestic linear pay-TV subscribers. Advertising revenues fell by 8% to $1.98 billion, while content revenues dropped 25% to $1.87 billion, attributed to lower box office and home entertainment revenues. These declines were influenced by last year's strong theatrical releases.
news.headline: Warner Bros. Discovery, Inc. stock surges after Q1 performance, despite net loss of $453 million and 9% decline in revenue.

news.summary: Frontdoor, Inc., a top home warranty provider, disclosed that its CEO Bill Cobb and CFO Jessica Ross are scheduled to participate in the J.P. Morgan 53rd Annual Global Technology, Media and Communications Conference. They will be engaged in a fireside chat on May 13, 2025, at 3:50pm EST. The discussion will be available via a live webcast (audio only) on the event's website. Frontdoor, Inc. is listed on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol FTDR.
news.headline: Frontdoor's CEO and CFO to attend J.P. Morgan conference on May 13, 2025. Live webcast at 3:50pm EST.

The report indicates that Seattle is a hot spot for travelers this season. The report includes various destinations and trends for upcoming summer travel.
news.headline: Allianz Partners USA's data shows beaches and big cities are top destinations for US summer travelers.

news.summary: US Foods Holding Corp. (NYSE: USFD) saw an increase in shares after releasing their first-quarter results. The company reported adjusted earnings per share of 68 cents, slightly below the analyst consensus of 69 cents. Their quarterly sales reached $9.35 billion, marking a 4.5% increase from the previous year, though falling short of the Street's expectation of $9.38 billion. Sysco experienced a 1.1% growth in total case volume, with a notable 2.5% boost from independent restaurants. Gross profit also saw a healthy rise, reaching $1.6 billion, up by 8%. Net income showed a substantial surge of 40.2%, hitting $115 million. For more details, the full article can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: US Foods' net income increased by 40.2%, reaching $115 million. The company plans a $1 billion buyback as shares rise.

This annual event acknowledges the accomplishments of 20 exceptional women in the Dallas business realm for their dedication to driving technological advancements in the area. With an impressive 25 years of experience in both the technology and hospitality fields, Reddy stands out as a seasoned professional making significant contributions to her industry.
news.headline: Suchi Reddy, Chief Information Officer at Visual Matrix, honored by Dallas Business Journal's Women in Technology Awards.

news.summary: XRP experienced a surge on May 8, driven by positive sentiment following Donald Trump's teased trade deal with the UK. The number of addresses holding 10,000+ XRP has been steadily increasing, indicating whale accumulation during price dips. XRP broke above a key resistance level, with a predicted rally towards $2.80-$3.66. XRP's price gained 7.50% to reach an intraday high of $2.27. Trump's trade deal announcement with the UK boosted risk appetite, reflected in a rise in S&P 500 futures and the broader crypto market. The 52-week correlation coefficient between XRP and the S&P 500 index has grown. Large holders accumulating XRP signals confidence for future gains. XRP's ongoing rally, breaking key resistance, suggests a potential 30% surge towards $2.80. A breakout above the upper trendline might push XRP towards $3.66. However, falling below $2.20 could lead to declines towards the 200-day EMA at $2.00. Adherence to technical analysis and risk management is advised in investment decisions.
news.headline: XRP surged 7.50% on May 8 to $2.27 amid trade deal buzz with the UK. Whale accumulation seen with rise in 10K+ XRP addresses. Bullish rally towards $2.80-$3.66 anticipated as XRP breaks key resistance. TradingView info reveals growing S&P correlation for XRP. Large holders confident for further gains. Whales continue to stack XRP despite pullbacks. Analysts predict an upside for XRP if it holds above $2.20 support. Editorial does not offer investment advice.

news.summary: Bitcoin price surged above $100,000, hitting $101,707, supported by positive regulatory and financial developments. US President Trump's trade deal announcement with the UK hinted at tariff reductions, leading to market optimism. The Dow rose 500 points, S&P 500 up 1.47%. Bitcoin traded around $101,600. Notably, $241 million in futures market liquidations initially contributed to the rally. Recent state bills in the US making Bitcoin reserves legal, and OCC confirming banks can trade crypto, indicate growing support. FDIC guideline allows banks to hold crypto assets. Bitcoin treasury and ETF inflows surged. Investors keen on BTC holding $100,000 support level for sustained growth. Market analysts emphasize monitoring BTC movements closely. Trump hinted at more trade deals, boosting market confidence. Bitcoin's return to six-figure territory signals improved investment and political environment since February. This reaffirms positive growth prospects for cryptocurrencies amidst evolving regulatory and market landscapes. Note: This summary does not offer investment advice. Investors should conduct individual research before making decisions.
news.headline: Bitcoin surpasses $101K amid US strategic reserve bill passing, BTC adoption surges. Market confident $100K will serve as strong support.

news.summary: Connecting Patients with Specialized Care ANN ARBOR, Mich., May 8, 2025 - The Sarcoma Alliance for Research through Collaboration (SARC) has announced the release of an updated SARC Sarcoma Centers Directory (SCD). This directory offers extensive information on specialized care centers for sarcoma patients, providing access to key resources. The revitalized SCD now includes a wider range of facilities, with a total of 150 sarcoma centers across the country, up from the previous 100. This expansion aims to bridge gaps in patient care and improve accessibility to specialized treatments.
news.headline: SARC introduces enhanced Sarcoma Centers Directory at upcoming meeting, bridging gaps between patients and specialized care.

news.summary: Toronto Hydro Corporation reported its financial and operating results for the first quarter ending March 31, 2025. The distribution revenue stood at $240.2 million, up $15.0 million from the same period in 2024 due to higher distribution rates and electricity consumption. Net income after regulatory balances saw a significant increase to $53.2 million, a $19.3 million rise from the prior year, driven by higher revenue and lower net credit amounts, despite increased expenses. Capital expenditures also surged to $244.7 million, up $45.2 million, attributed to customer connections and infrastructure replacements. James Hinds resigned from the Board of Directors on March 19 for personal reasons. Full story on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Toronto Hydro Corp. reported Q1 2025 financial results: $240.2M distribution revenue, $53.2M net income, $244.7M capex.

news.summary: In Los Angeles on May 8, 2025, Gravity, the sole American OEM independent EVSE hardware and software developer, revealed their latest charging technology. The 500kW charging system, almost twice as fast as the closest competitors in LA, stands out for its speed. Their charging sites are uniquely designed with Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) capabilities to enhance resiliency. Gravity also introduced the industry's first human-less charging system tailored for autonomous vehicles.
news.headline: Gravity to introduce 5-minute EV charging at 8 LA sites, 500kW - twice as fast as rivals. Includes V2G technology and autonomous vehicle charging.

news.summary: On May 8, 2025, in St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, Fortis Inc. held its Annual Meeting of Shareholders. The company (TSX/NYSE: FTS) disclosed that shareholders unanimously supported all agenda items. This included the election of nominated directors, the appointment of Deloitte LLP as auditors, and the endorsement of the Corporation's executive compensation approach through a non-binding advisory vote. This decisive outcome underscores strong investor confidence in Fortis' leadership and financial stewardship.
news.headline: Fortis Inc. shareholders approve directors, auditors, and executive compensation in annual meeting.

news.summary: In Scottsdale, Arizona, Nextiva will be celebrating its 17th anniversary on May 15th by marking the inaugural Customer Experience Day. This event aims to highlight the role of AI-driven technology in transforming customer interaction. Nextiva, a prominent player in unified customer experience management, will emphasize the significance of reinventing customer engagement methods. Customer Experience Day will be held annually on May 15th, recognizing individuals shaping the future of customer relations.
news.headline: Nextiva launches Customer Experience Day on May 15, their 17th anniversary, celebrating AI-driven technology enhancing customer engagement globally.
